title: The Station Where the Attendant Waits Sideways

features.name: the still-glowing pumps
features.desc-on-touch: Red numbers frozen mid-transaction. The decimal points glow in an amber that does not match any year's electricity. They read prices in currency that stopped existing in 1984.

features.name: the shop-window
features.desc-on-touch: An attendant stands there, profile three-quarter, neither fully facing the window nor facing the station. Your attention slides off them like they are standing in a gap between moments.

features.name: the register-counter
features.desc-on-touch: Formica. Scratch marks in shapes that mean nothing now. The cash drawer is open but holds only receipts from the day someone forgot to close it. That day was November 14th, 1983.

features.name: the car-park-shapes
features.desc-on-touch: Oil stains in the shape of vehicles that no longer exist. The archive compares the stain-patterns to the Boxing Historian's footprints and finds them the same size. The cars and the historian arrived together.

inscription: the station is still open, still waiting to complete a transaction from forty-two years ago

file.name: ServiceStation_Dusk_NSW_1983_Records.txt
file.note: Attendant log. Hours are listed but the attendant is still there in the window. The log ends mid-sentence: 'Customer arrived and I turned to face them but something in the angle of the light prevented

exits.label: back down the lane toward the boxing historian's yard
toward the road you can hear but have never seen
